title: DUMPSTER BEAR
blurb: Behind Lazzaro's. Sanitation called it a 'big raccoon.' The shift super hasn't been to work in three days. Bring the revolver and the matches.
briefing: The dumpster behind Lazzaro's deli is breathing. The Pawnbroker hands you the revolver — six rounds, one for the head when you're sure it's dead. The thing inside has been eating long enough that it has forgotten what it used to be. Burn the work order on the way home.
epigraph: "You put six rounds into it and one more into its head when you are sure it is dead, because you are not sure it is dead."

debrief.win: The shift super at Lazzaro's came back to work this morning. Asked nobody anything. Sanitation is taking the dumpster to the landfill in Staten Island. The work order is burning in my ashtray. Eat. You earned it.
debrief.loss: I will take the revolver back. I'll oil it and put it in the case. The kid I send next will not be told what was in there. Some things, only the first one through the door has to know.
debrief.sGrade: Six rounds and one for the head, exactly the way I taught your father. He would be proud, in his way. He would also tell you to never, ever go behind Lazzaro's again.
